mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through reduced aggregate demand and profit margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130910k as of Dec 2009 reflects post-crisis employment recovery plateau
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market slack
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS declines do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of instances over a 3-year walk-forward window, or Rank IC < 0.04 across all rolling 12-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero (0.12%) combined with sustained low inflation (CPIAUCSL growth < 2% annualized) signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity risk premiums and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% and CPIAUCSL at 217.541 as of Dec 2009
regime_hint: zero-bound monetary policy with subdued inflation
failure_condition: Positive 60-day SPX returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or the coefficient on the interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strengthening consumer demand and corporate revenue growth, which drives positive earnings revisions and S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism reflects real-time demand signals that precede formal earnings announcements.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 352985M as of Dec 2009 shows post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS growth does not precede positive 20-day SPX returns in > 58% of instances, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS growth and forward 20-day returns is < 0.08 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining below trend signals weakening economic capacity utilization and future profit pressure, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors front-run earnings downgrades. The mechanism operates through rapid repricing of cyclical equities.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO prints do not precede negative 5-day SPX returns in > 52% of triggered instances, or the predictive power decays to Rank IC < 0.02 in any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer confidence and construction sector activity. When both rise together, they indicate broad-based demand expansion that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures coordinated demand strength.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 574k and RSAFS at 352985M as of Nov-Dec 2009
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with synchronized demand rebound
failure_condition: Positive 20-day SPX returns occur in < 54% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient (HOUST × RSAFS) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) at elevated levels (> 9.5%) signals persistent labor market slack that constrains wage growth and inflation, allowing the Fed to maintain accommodative policy. This regime supports equity valuations through lower discount rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 10.0% and FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of Dec 2009
regime_hint: high-unemployment low-rate regime supporting risk assets
failure_condition: Positive 60-day SPX returns occur in < 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (UNRATE × FEDFUNDS) shows negative or insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained low inflation (CPIAUCSL 12-month growth < 1.5%) combined with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS month-over-month < -0.2%) signals deflationary pressure and labor market weakness that forces the Fed to maintain zero rates, supporting equity risk appetite and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 12-month CPIAUCSL growth < 1.5% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change < -0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.541 and PAYEMS at 130910k as of Dec 2009
regime_hint: deflationary shock with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: Positive 20-day SPX returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) rising above trend combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth signals strengthening productive capacity and labor utilization, indicating robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.3043 and PAYEMS at 130910k as of Dec 2009
regime_hint: synchronized supply-side and labor expansion in recovery phase
failure_condition: Positive 60-day SPX returns occur in < 56% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient (INDPRO × PAYEMS) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in any 2-year rolling regression.
